differentials.name: severe pain + limited joint motion (bone may encroach on nerves, blood vessels, or joints) → ± joint contracture
differentials.raw: severe pain + limited joint motion (bone may encroach on nerves, blood vessels, or joints) → ± joint contracture

differentials.name: thickening + fibrosis of overlying skin
differentials.raw: thickening + fibrosis of overlying skin (resembling scleroderma); muscle atrophy (frequent)

differentials.name: Dermatofibrosis lenticularis disseminata (Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome) = asymptomatic small flesh-colored to yellow dermal papules / coalescent plaques (in 25%) on buttocks, trunk, arms, skin folds
differentials.raw: Dermatofibrosis lenticularis disseminata (Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome) = asymptomatic small flesh-colored to yellow dermal papules / coalescent plaques (in 25%) on buttocks, trunk, arms, skin folds

differentials.name: Fibrous dysplasia
differentials.raw: Fibrous dysplasia (normal bone structure not lost, not as dense)

differentials.name: Hyperostosis of neurofibromatosis, tuberous sclerosis, hemangiomas
differentials.raw: Hyperostosis of neurofibromatosis, tuberous sclerosis, hemangiomas

context: =  LÉRI DISEASE [melos, Greek = limb; rhein, Greek = flow] [André Léri (1875-1930), pupil of Joseph Babinski and Pierre Marie, president of French Ophthalmological Society] =  rare sporadic mixed sclerosing bone dysplasia with disturbances in endochondral + intramembranous ossification characteristically appearing as flowing hyperostosis Age:   usually occult until adulthood; slow chronic progressive course in adults; rapid progression in children Cause:   developmental error of intramembranous bone formation related to mutation in LEMD3 on chromosome 12q, which codes for an inner nuclear memb
